stories.headline: Shapiro pressure forces PECO to withdraw rate case that would have hit 1.7M customers
stories.summary: PECO pulled its pending PUC filing that would have raised the average residential electric bill ~$20.08/month and gas bill ~$14.52/month starting 2027, after Shapiro sent an April 29 letter to 24 utility CEOs vowing to 'vocally and forcefully' oppose hikes.

stories.headline: FERC's December co-location order forces PJM tariff rewrite — directly bearing on Susquehanna
stories.summary: FERC's Dec. 18, 2025 order found PJM's tariff 'unjust and unreasonable' for co-located load and ordered three new transmission services (Interim NITS, Firm and Non-Firm Contract Demand) plus a BTMG resource-adequacy threshold — the framework Talen-Amazon now has to operate under.

stories.headline: PJM 27/28 BRA cleared at $333.44/MW-day cap — still 6,623 MW short of reliability requirement
stories.summary: December's auction procured 134,479 MW UCAP (43% gas, 21% nuclear, 20% coal, 5% DR) at the FERC-approved cap, up 1.3% from the 26/27 result; peak load forecast jumped ~5,250 MW year-over-year, with ~5,100 MW of that from data centers.

stories.headline: NRECA pushes Congress to lift RUS electric-loan authority by 50% for FY27
stories.summary: RUS Administrator Karl Elmshaeuser told NRECA's Legislative Conference the agency is again notifying Congress it will lend up to 25% above the program's $7B FY26 cap; NRECA wants statutory headroom raised to 50% as co-op load growth keeps outpacing infrastructure.

stories.headline: Building-trades unions become public ally for tech giants on data-center buildout
stories.summary: Trades unions are scrambling to recruit apprentices for hyperscaler data-center projects and now echo the China-AI-race talking point, giving Microsoft, Amazon and Google a blue-collar voice in zoning and siting fights — including in PA where Bucks County is the latest flashpoint.

stories.headline: Today is the final day to register to vote in PA's May 19 primary
stories.summary: Steve Ulrich's Playbook leads with the registration deadline for the closed primary; key downballot races include the GOP primary in PA-1 (Fitzpatrick) and competitive Dem primaries in SD-16 (Lehigh/Bucks) where Bradley Merkl-Gump and Mark Pinsley are running to face Sen. Jarrett Coleman.

stories.headline: Politico floats Fetterman party-switch scenario if Senate balance is razor-thin in November
stories.summary: Politico's piece — circulated in Monday's PoliticsPA Playbook — games out a post-election scenario where Trump and Senate Republicans pressure Fetterman to switch or go independent if Democrats net the four seats they need; Fetterman publicly says he's 'staying' a Democrat.

stories.headline: Bucks County data-center fights become a 2026 election issue
stories.summary: Multiple pending data-center projects across Bucks municipalities are pulling township and county candidates into resident-vs-developer disputes — a leading edge of the broader PA siting/zoning fight that Allegheny and PREA distribution co-ops are watching as load applications spread.

stories.headline: Axios report alleges Shapiro quietly aided Garrity's 2024 Treasurer reelection over Dem Erin McClelland
stories.summary: PA-7 Dem candidate Bob Brooks, on a recording obtained by Axios, told college students Shapiro helped GOP Treasurer Stacy Garrity after McClelland criticized him as a potential VP pick — a story that could complicate Shapiro's standing with state Dems heading into May 19.

stories.headline: Southern redistricting frenzy after Callais — LA suspends primaries, AL preps special session
stories.summary: Gov. Jeff Landry suspended Louisiana's already-begun House primaries to let Republicans redraw maps post-Callais; Alabama's Ivey called a special session pending a SCOTUS injunction lift; Tennessee, Florida and South Carolina are also in motion, with 12+ House seats potentially in play.

stories.headline: Trump's 5,000-troop pullout from Germany draws bipartisan rebuke from Armed Services chairs
stories.summary: Senate Armed Services Chair Roger Wicker and House Chair Mike Rogers issued a joint statement saying they are 'very concerned'; Sen. Ruben Gallego said NDAA Section 1249 requires presidential certifications before drawdowns below 76,000 troops in Europe and Dems plan to invoke it.

stories.headline: ICE-thanks line in June 2025 Boulder resolution roils Senate Dem primaries
stories.summary: Reps. Haley Stevens (MI), Angie Craig (MN), Raja Krishnamoorthi (IL) and April McClain Delaney (MD) are taking incoming from primary opponents over their votes for the resolution's final line thanking ICE — a vote 75 House Democrats joined and that Sen. McMorrow weaponized at an AFT forum Saturday.

stories.headline: Indiana and Ohio hold primaries Tuesday — OH-9 GOP race the marquee fight to take on Kaptur
stories.summary: Former state Rep. Derek Merrin (the 2024 nominee) is favored over state Rep. Josh Williams and former ICE official Madison Sheahan in the GOP primary to challenge Marcy Kaptur; Trump is also targeting Indiana state senators who blocked the redistricting push.

stories.headline: Pirates sweep Reds with 1-0 walk-off, Cruz singles in Griffin to push to 19-16
stories.summary: Rookie SS Konnor Griffin doubled off Tony Santillan with two outs in the 8th and Oneil Cruz singled him in; Braxton Ashcraft turned in a no-decision gem, Gregory Soto (3-0) got the last four outs, and Reds starter Chase Burns lowered his ERA to 2.20 in seven-plus.

stories.headline: Steelers OTAs open May 18 under McCarthy with shorter blocks than Tomlin era
stories.summary: First-year HC Mike McCarthy's offseason program starts a week earlier than 2025 and never groups more than two OTAs in a row (May 18, 20-21, 26, 28-29; June 8-9, 11-12); the team also placed a UFA tender on Aaron Rodgers, which insiders frame as protective rather than active.

stories.headline: Penguins enter offseason with 16 UFAs and Dubas weighing Malkin, Skinner futures
stories.summary: After a first-round playoff exit to the Flyers, GM Kyle Dubas faces decisions on Evgeni Malkin (likely a short-term legacy deal) and starter Stuart Skinner (could walk with Sergei Murashov and Joel Blomqvist in the system); the Pens are expected to lean on their stockpiled draft capital.
